title: Streaming Sitemap XML, Index Graphs, and Admission Bounds · Runtime Contract: Sitemap Provenance

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Retain source digest, index chain, entry position, raw loc, resolved key, decision, and rejection cause.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Sitemap Provenance model · Theory Sitemap Provenance, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Sitemap Provenance precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Sitemap Provenance model · Theory Sitemap Provenance, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory Go team handles case 5388 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to stream bounded sitemap XML and index graphs through host, scope, hint, and provenance admission; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then reorder one origin, retry, channel, context, goroutine, Colly callback, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain source digest, index chain, entry position, raw loc, resolved key, decision, and rejection cause.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Sitemap Provenance model · Theory Sitemap Provenance, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Final URL alone explains sitemap discovery.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The cumulative go portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: crawler-go-sitemap-provenance-14kbfb.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-go-sitemap-provenance-14kbfb(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_crawler_go_sitemap_provenance_14kbfb\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?sitemapEntries < 15)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?accountedEntries < 14 \|\| accountedEntries > sitemapEntries)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?indexCases < 5)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unboundedEdges > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using retain and source plus specific case evidence.
